note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the fabrication and operation of acoustofluidic devices designed to support bulk acoustic standing waves for sheathless focusing of particles. The goal is to create and test these devices using photolithography, deep reactive ion etching, and other techniques.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "Photolithography: A mask aligner is used to expose a photoresist film.",
    "Deep Reactive Ion Etching: A wafer is etched using a deep reactive ion etching machine.",
    "Electrical Testing: A multimeter measures electrical properties of the fabricated device.",
    "Operating the Acoustofluidic Device: An oscilloscope displays waveforms generated by the PZT (Piezoelectric Transducer)."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Photoresist film",
    "Wafer",
    "PZT (Piezoelectric Transducer)",
    "Multimeter",
    "Oscilloscope"
  ],

    "Oscilloscope"
  ],

    "Flow rate data shown in graphs"
  ],

    "Transition from photolithography to deep reactive ion etching",
    "Transition from electrical testing to operating the acoustofluidic device"
